---
title: "Bivariate location-coscale models"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Bivariate location-coscale models}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This article introduces the first flagship `drmTMB` model: bivariate
location-scale regression with predictor-dependent residual correlation.

Current fixed-effect syntax:

```r
drmTMB(
  bf(
    mu1 = y1 ~ x1 + x2,
    mu2 = y2 ~ x1,
    sigma1 = ~ z1,
    sigma2 = ~ z2,
    rho12 = ~ w
  ),
  family = biv_gaussian(),
  data = dat
)
```

The central scientific question is whether predictors change trait coupling,
not only trait means or trait variances.

The likelihood is:

```text
[y1_i, y2_i]' ~ MVN([mu1_i, mu2_i]', Omega_i)
Omega_i[1,1] = sigma1_i^2
Omega_i[2,2] = sigma2_i^2
Omega_i[1,2] = rho12_i * sigma1_i * sigma2_i
rho12_i = tanh(X_rho12 beta_rho12)
```

Minimal runnable example:

```r
set.seed(1)
n <- 300
dat <- data.frame(x = rnorm(n), z1 = rnorm(n), z2 = rnorm(n), w = rnorm(n))
mu1 <- 0.2 + 0.5 * dat$x
mu2 <- -0.1 + 0.4 * dat$x
sigma1 <- exp(-0.2 + 0.2 * dat$z1)
sigma2 <- exp(0.1 - 0.2 * dat$z2)
rho12 <- tanh(-0.1 + 0.4 * dat$w)
e1 <- rnorm(n)
e2 <- rho12 * e1 + sqrt(1 - rho12^2) * rnorm(n)
dat$y1 <- mu1 + sigma1 * e1
dat$y2 <- mu2 + sigma2 * e2

fit <- drmTMB(
  bf(
    mu1 = y1 ~ x,
    mu2 = y2 ~ x,
    sigma1 = ~ z1,
    sigma2 = ~ z2,
    rho12 = ~ w
  ),
  family = biv_gaussian(),
  data = dat
)

coef(fit, "rho12")
predict(fit, dpar = "rho12")[1:5]
```

The middle `p` in `(1 | p | id)` follows brms-style grouping for correlated
group-level effects. That random-effect syntax is planned but not implemented
yet. It is distinct from residual `rho12`.
